front: What is a universal quantifier? What is its symbol?
back: A universal quantifier expresses that a predicate is true for every element in a domain. Its symbol is ∀.

front: What is an existential quantifier? What is its symbol?
back: An existential quantifier expresses that a predicate is true for at least one element in a domain. Its symbol is ∃.

front: What is the domain (or universe) of discourse in the context of quantifiers?
back: The domain of discourse is the set from which the values of the variables in a quantified statement are chosen.

front: What is the negation of the universal statement ∀x P(x)?
back: The negation is ∃x ¬P(x). It asserts that there is at least one x for which P(x) is false.

front: What is the negation of the existential statement ∃x Q(x)?
back: The negation is ∀x ¬Q(x). It asserts that Q(x) is false for every element x in the domain.
